Comment: Oh, dear.  Precisely 3:21 too long.
Why?  Why did they make this song?  Why would anyone want to listen to it?  A pointless song for a pointless round.
I suppose it's not completely horrible, within the slim context of music that 14 year old girls might like.  Is Jenny on holiday with "Emily in Paris", if you know what I'm saying?
Aww, too bad we already had the animal round.  I can kinda see what you mean about his voice, but this is supposed to convince us of his songwriting talent?

I liked aspects of this three-songs-in-one, but it doesn't quite add up for me.  And we had Caroline Polachek recently.  One extra theme point simply because blood orange could conceivably be used in perfumes?
Some good lyrics, then a lot of repetition of lyrics, which is not great.

I wanted to give some love to these Detroit homies, but couldn't get over the blatant unremarkableness of the smooth funk.  You were headed for a downvote.
Luckily enough for you, however, the line "The air was filled with suspicion" could be said to refer to an actual product, Sardeau Suspicion Parfum:
https://www.collectorsweekly.com/stories/183648-suspicion-perfume
Therefore, IMHO, your song hits closest to the theme's bullseye, so I gave you 3 extra points.
Hard dance music with window-shaking bass is so not my thing.  But the song does contain orange and banana, so instead of a -2, you get a -1.
Don't really care for his voice or most of the music, but the chorus squeaks it into a positive score.  His techno-optimism will surely fade once AI crashes our economies.

You should get some kind of award for your reasoning, if only the theme turned out to be ticking as many of the previous rounds' boxes as possible.  
Was not familiar with the original.  Dolores is a hard act to follow, so distinctive is her voice.  A capable cover (but not "better than the original"-- whoops, ya missed one).
Heard this enough, can't bear to listen to it again.
Nice and bouncy.
I am liking them fat-ass gee-tars.  Reminiscent of Red House Painters and a slowed-down version of mediocre 80's rockers like Billy Squier or something (but in a good way).
The verses were not really my thing, but I was able to enjoy the 80's-style chorus a bit.  Two extra theme points for mentioning a "potion"!
Love the trashed-out yet still-beautiful mood.  I was intrigued when I read a review of this album when it came out, and I think I listened to one song, but didn't follow up on it any further.
Quality punky-pop.

Oops, I misheard "oat" for "oak"-- there are no trees.
But "Blended flower" and "roses"-- those count toward the theme as common perfume ingredients, right?

Spy in the House is for the James Bond marathon-lover who feels stuck in a sexless marriage, resulting in elaborate daytime fantasies about the neighbor next door, who only kind of resembles Pussy Galore. Smells of Vesper martini, musk, amber, cigars, deception, cold showers, and blow-up dolls. 

The more I listen to this, the catchier it gets, but I also feel like I’ve been smacked a little too hard in the face with the 80s. Had to have been there.

mi buena suerte smells like fizzy cola, strawberry lipgloss and Cholula hot sauce. You smelled it on a girl walking by at the mall and you wished you stopped to get to know/smell her better because she could have been “the one” that changed your life. 

My Spanish is crap and I struggled to make out/find the lyrics, darn.

Dulling the Horns smells like desert mountain air, cows, moonshine, sage (advice), and heartbreak. The signature scent of those who live alone under a wide open sky because they’ve just seen too much.

The Signals perfume and cologne set is marketed using a video ad inspired by “The Terminator” except it’s a sexy woman coming from the future to save your ass, asserting that you must “come with me if you want to live!” before dragging you to the dance floor. She smells like 1985’s Poison by Christian Dior, and warm poptarts.

I think I know who submitted this and I’ve been contemplating why I’ve downvoted you often, and it’s because I think you are at the cutting edge of where my music taste needs to grow, this is a compliment.

World Go to Hell appropriately smells like burning leaves, salty tears, wine, dusty attics, and the shoe pile display at the Auschwitz museum, in a most tragically beautiful way. For the discerning realist well-versed in history who feels most alive when they are sad. 

I think this is lovely!!

I Know You Love Me is a punchy body spray smelling of combat boots, plums, hazelnuts, and angostura bitters. The intense scent only lasts as long as the mosh pit does, but you do think about the scent-wearing girl who left the club wearing your winter jacket for days after. She’s totally into you, yeah? You’ll totally get your coat back when you see her again. Totally.

The Quiet Joys of Brotherhood is a deeply warm, spicy, earthy unisex scent—vanilla, patchouli, wildflowers, shamrocks, oak, and seawater. Leaves a lasting impression. Target audience: romantic introverts, the gay couples on Heated Rivalry, and all the queers who are binge watching it right now. Love-Hated by the red-pilled incels who secretly can’t wait to tenderly huff this off of their Grindr dates.

Silliness aside this is a gorgeous song with lovely lyrics.

Synthesizer is worn by the fearless baddie with blue hair, a spiky tongue piercing, and a dinosaur tattoo at the roller rink. They are lost in their own world, unless you have a clove cigarette you’re willing to spare. Strong sweet notes of fruity gum, but also pennies, cardamom, and hot summer 70s car vinyl.

Let the Fireflies Fly Away is a quirky scent designed to intrigue the true connoisseur of dissociation, who is literally or figuratively always trying to get away, far far away, from here. Smells like a greasy spoon diner, a musky zoo enclosure, giant Devonian ferns, black currants, chicken coop, and squashed bugs…but like, in a good way.

Every Ounce of Me is for the self-actualized girlie who is working on herself after some dusty fucker drained her like an energy vampire. She knows her worth now and though she looks real kind and down to earth, she’ll cut your ass up. Sweet and floral with notes of honey cake, lilacs, jasmine, razorblades, and, rather suspiciously, almonds.

Medicine For Horses smells like ivermectin, spinal fluid, peaches, formaldehyde, and wheat. Popular among partners of horse girls, who are trying to catch their attention because they love the horses more than them.

Recycle Bin by Tom Ford.  Smells expensive, but paradoxically like melted plastic, sour milk, and hints of cardboard soaked with pizza grease that shouldn’t have been put in the bin, but someone did anyway. The person who wears this thinks you will never understand them. 

Song is hilarious and this is a downvote avec apologies.

Is That All There Is? is the perfume industry’s answer to some modern women’s decision to pursue entire lives off outside traditional beaten paths. Smells like tart cherry, tuberose, moss, chocolate cake, freshly-bathed cats, fresh ink on divorce papers, and sleeping in on the weekends.

Disappointment…”because you can’t spell disappointment without men” is a wildly popular cologne and aftershave set. It first hits with pleasant notes of sandalwood and citrus. Alas, as you come closer—distinct notes of stale beer, musty basement, and Doritos. After brief engagement you leave the exchange…disappointed, catching final topnotes of literal bullshit and crusty jizz sock. Scent lingers much like the odor of a seafood market with health code violations.

I Didn’t Come Here For Art smells like cotton candy, latex, dirty linen, leather, lube, and sweaty balls. The person who wears this is just in it for the plot so get out of their fucking way, okay?

I think this is hilarious and I am sad I downvoted it. Avec beaucoup apologies.

Hooting and Howling is high-end cologne for sophisticated, brooding hooligans whose dating profiles state they are looking for a “ride or die.” Smells like cedar, silk, dirt, gunpowder, pinewood coffins, guitar strings, and licorice.

Mind Loaded is a modern scent for the people lost in thought, who go out on Fridays hoping to catch the eye of their ex at the corner bar (because they think they might have made a mistake dumping them six months ago cuz life has lost all meaning, but they dunno if they should say anything because it would be weird now and they look happy, how awkward). Distinct notes of vetiver, blood orange, petrichor, and tobacco.
A band I should see green hearts in their Spotify discography, but no.  Like this a lot, will do further research.
What a good album this is.  Rich like an Italian wedding soup.  https://cooking.nytimes.com/recipes/1024492-italian-wedding-soup  YUM.

Obviously the winner.  Well done, me.  Stay with me as I Vizzini the heck out of this.

As the primary purpose of cologne by 22 year-old men is to attract predators when lost in the woods, there will be much hooting and howling by said predators in pursuit of their prey.

Armaf Club de Nuit Intense, the aroma of found footage capturing twigs snapping and throaty growls near camp. Dior Sauvage, the scent of a messy death by bears. Or Sasquatch.  Grisly, man.  Acqua di Gio, what the discerning nose appreciates during trampling by water buffaloes, well-known to gallivant in the trees.  Creed, splashed liberally on neck and wrists prior to their violent end from wolves, as deserved by Scott Stapp, or anyone wearing a scent with that name.  

Case, rested.

Born To Laugh At Tornadoes being a beloved college album, was surprised and pleased at What Up, Dog? booming their popularity.  Stupid DVs.  Need to place.  Feels like a round where I'll fret about needing to use DVs, submit, and find my own choice tanking.
This is fantastic.  Winner in Best Facial Covering category. Tough competition from Glass Beads, Orville Peck, Kneecap, and Slipknot.  "New Boyfriend", after one listen, also excellent.

Interesting.  First hearing this. I've heard Blondshell, but the intonation on this aligned so much with Dolores, it's new and lovely.  Also, "Disappointment" is the perfect cologne for AMC fans.  This is resisting my DV placement.

What more do you want?  Is your latte over 160°F (70°C)?  Is there a draft in the room? Would you like a blanket? Do you miss candy bars for a quarter? Why are you taking your toys away? Are you not entertained?
Red Sovine's "Teddy Bear" immediately came to mind. I don't know what that says about me.
ML introduced me to this group.  One of those bands better than their name would suggest.  There's not a lot of distance between this song and The National, really.
New to me.  Nice how LEG, Wet Leg, and others appear as somewhat novelty and stick around for their talent.
Well, now.  Chickens.  That's worth several votes.
Can't DV this with your opening comment.  More love to you.  Not my usual cup of tea, but I hear the ocean and the wind, and if Martin McDonagh directs, an Oscar nominated picture.
Crunchy guitars.  Yum yum.  This round esp. there aren't any songs that are pushing me to DV.  Competition-based DVs and apologies.

Fantastic.  Love this, and should have heard it earlier. Always new music to hear.  Holy cow - only $5 AUD? Sold! https://bleaksquad.bandcamp.com/album/strange-love

Cherry Ghost's People Help the People leaps (leapt? leaped?) to mind.  https://open.spotify.com/track/6ahKIw4OP1VEprkJu7XHiQ

Have and love each of their four albums.  Beautiful and pure pop. Missing the two EPs: Face to Face on High Places (2007) and Put Your Sad Down (2012).  Fallon is the nadir of late night hosts, but their appearance on the show is a highlight: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Ci1DKSzitM4
Had this album in my hands earlier today.  Venn intersection!  What else do we like together?
Godofredo did that for me last year.  The fun translates well.
Efficient, but somehow I'm not quite moved.

Sometimes when a song progresses pretty much exactly as you’re expecting, it feels painfully predictable, but sometimes it’s a joy. This is the latter. Why is that? Muy bien! I tried to find out more about them but there are quite a few bands called "feel trip", who knew?
Great bass riff/sound straight up. Learning to let go is one of life’s most valuable skills, I’m realising of late. This feels a bit half-built though.
Oy, the downvotes were hard to place. Just a bit underwhelmed.
Love how this gradually climbs from torpid despair to glorious agitation, then falls back.

Yes please, I will always lap up the hippy-dippy-trippy stuff, especially with a folky spine. The drones in this are so gorgeous, I'll forgive the slight vocal wonkiness. Probably whiffs of patchouli in the studio here.
Odourless but ok. I don't think I've heard Electric Six before, but I just this day noticed a poster for them playing locally next month. Howboutdat?

Fireflies are *always* a theme, so nice one there. I like a song with lots of animal references too. Zoos invariably have a lot of pungent aromas, but I feel that's different to cosmetic smells, so no joy there.
Oh... I loved that Let's Eat Grandma song, but this is just straight 80s pastiche, isn't it? Sorry.
God, it annoys me to enjoy a band called Viagra Boys as much as I like this. Sounds a bit like Interpol having a menty-B. Unscented.

I could write a boring amount of words about the reasons this hits home, a very dull domestic rage born of sharing recycle bins with a bunch of people for many years. Suffice to say, hell yeah Neil! This sort of thing doesn’t exactly invite repeat listening, but hell yeah just the same. Oh, but how could you fail to mention that he also covered Hula Maiden on this album?

Well played. Hearing something like this thrown in the mix, it's a whole other level. Lyrically, it's incredible. She no doubt recorded this while heavily scented. I probably won't be the only one to mention that Trump named this his favourite song back in pre-POTUS days. Enlightening.
I'm on board for good Cranberries done well. No scents, perfumes, toilet waters detected.

Grew on me a lot - the snark, the vocal delivery, the beats, the build. Hits the same spot as the best Brat tracks. And their look! Amazing. This club probably reeks of scents applied one spray too many. Points.
Not Was (Not Was)! File under "I don't really know why this band annoys me but they do". Couldn't find another one deserving a downvote so had to drop a double, with apologies.
Nice turns of phrase and vocals.
Blood orange is definitely a perfume note - well (accidentally) done. The song is pretty good, lots of interesting turns, even if it seems kind of a sketch.

i was going to submit the viagra boys in the Scandinavian round in a previous league. great band. you've pandered a little to perceived league tastes imo as this is not the standard viagra boys sound but very good nevertheless.
made me smile. a splash of colour in greyish playlist.

what an apt title. ok pedants, to be disappointed i must have had a pre conceived notion of something better which, as an unfamiliar, to me, artist i did not. maybe just disappointment that it didn't leave more of an impression.

love lynks although more as a live spectacle than recorded output. always tremendous fun. seen lynks many times from when i was stalwart of the south london scene pre-covid. unexpected choice submitter, fabulous departure from the league norm.
points not points. daytime radio for bored housewives and househusbands.
not enough howling and more akin to domestic pets than wild beasts. like it when it gets going though.
a certain charm but just so safe as a choice. have we lost our edge and settled for middle aged comfort listening? is there not more to music than this generic output?

love mark. but for someone who has mastered the art of penning beautifully crafted alt melancholic melodies this is far from his greatest moment. sounds thrown together as an album filler. appreciate this may have been chosen simply for the title and for that reason (and because it's MM) i applaud you.
i've oftened wondered, as electric six continue to exist and indeed tour , just who would ever listen to them.  i'm going to find out shortly.
having listened to this i am trembling at the thought of  having to listen to "the complete" vanguard recordings. so bland it's torture.
decent enough. popes by numbers.

this is almost certainly girvan. I bought this but haven't played it yet. I might be regretting buying it (still, it's a limited version with heart shaped bonus disc so I can aways sell it). it's ok. best lp of 2026? not a chance. probably wasn't on the 1st january either.
like this very much. a great fan of complementary vocal lines.
haven't listened to this album is a while but a nice reminder. good stuff.
a good listen.
love this. very good indeed.

‘Essex Honey’ could be a scent. I mean , it could also be a euphemism for something glutinous, sticky and produced from an orifice, but it COULD be a scent. 

Interesting song that makes me want to hear the album.
Struggling to relate this one to the theme. An album and band I like a lot. I hear a fair bit of Blue Nile (which probably IS a perfume) in the mix.
How about a combined shampoo/dry shampoo called Wash (Not Wash)?

For our US friends, Axe deodorant is called Lynx over here, meaning this is an accidentally brilliant choice, although I think it might be Michael O’Hara’s, the mischievous devil. 

The Almodovar at the bar line is brilliant.
I think all of us flies can get behind the scent of Disappointment.
I’ve never really liked this song much and so I’m relieved to say I can’t find a link to the theme.

Medicine! That’s as close to the theme as we’re going to get, people. Even if it is for horses. 

I watched their Glastonbury set last year or the year before and enjoyed. Don’t remember them sounding this much like The National.

Every Ounce of Me sounds like it could be a bathroom product of some kind, probably not one that I want to think about too much. 

This is a great album.

It has everything to cover the clandestine theme, except for something to do with perfume. 

(OK have a point for ‘jar’ in the lyrics). 

A song I was already fond of because of the excellent Mr Mulcahy sounding like he’s doing an ode to us. Think I put this on a mix CD or two back in the day.
So massively far from the theme that it’s got to be downvote o’clock.

All those keywords and none of them anything to do with perfume. 

Great history lesson and song though.
I would love you more if you’d included any reference to perfume.

I would probably buy a bathroom product called ‘Bleak Squad’. 

Excited by that line-up. Will be listening to this on full.

Great write-up, but the only connection I can find to a perfume is that the cover art might suit one. Worth a point I guess. 

I don’t  know this album but it sounds like something I’d like a lot.

No connection to the theme, and also not very enjoyable.
I can see what you mean here - I like how it sounds, but I also don't really know what it all means and "Viagra Boys" is just a bad name.
Always like to hear some Mark Mulcahy. Forgot about his wife dying and that tribute album.
Definitely a happy song, which I crave these days.
This is a bit mesmerizing on its own, but this story - Joan Baez, Pynchon? Off to fall down a rabbit hole reading about these folks.
1997 was a good year for songs like this.
Apt indeed. I like it!
So many dead people in this round with great songs.
This is fun.
I like the different voices and I might wear something called "Essex Honey."
This is a weird reaction, but I don't think the words "hooting and howling" for some reason.
Always fun.
Love this. Kind of "you'll dance to anything" but 30 yrs later.
Would wear 'Disappontment' - might not wear "Blondshell', and really appreciate this cover so much.
This is a weird song and it seems to be Trump's favorite. Poor thing (the song).
Like the recycle bin, this stresses me out
Sounds like something I already know and like, in a good way.
Infectious energy. Another stellar find this week.
This has some nice layers. I could listen to it while spacing out on the couch and sinking into a sunny nap. The last section is a little bit incongruous.
Well done track, though not something I am likely to return to. Just preferences.

Love this about the band from the wikipedia entry: "...a sausage factory of funk, rock, jazz and electronic dance music..." 

I appreciated the trip back to the 80's....  enhanced by the video link.
This brought several smiles in the room, but has a very specific place and time, doesn't it? Had to use one more downvote.
Solid cover.
Downvoted with apologies.... it just doesn't quite fit in my head this week.
My teenage son was in the room when this one came on, and boy did his ears perk up. I don't know, but I kind of liked this more than I probably should. The message is eternal too...
This is pretty far into pure pop territory for me, but I still enjoyed it. The world is grim enough... so I'm open to some sparkly sounds.
Well crafted. An extra point for the title.
This is an interesting combination of sounds. I'd probably not turn it on to listen to on my own, but it would be high on the list for a party playlist. Thanks for the new find.
I'm sure there is a mood where this would be perfect, but I was not in it.
The band is a familiar name that I don't know if I had heard before. Super fun to listen to, even if not particularly memorable.
Gorgeous, even with the big downshift from the preceding track. Killer band name. This is how you add a sharp edge to something pretty. Will check out the record.
Massive chorus turns an ok song into a really good song.
Give me all the slow fuzzy guitars and teach me to let go. I love this.
This is really good. Not a whiff of perfume, though.
There’s a little sweat in there, but no perfume to cover it up. I’m not sure about the song. It’s got something, but is that enough?

The air was filled with suspicion, not perume. And that orchestral hit is so peak 80’s that it’s from the 90’s. It’s not bad, but it’s not really for me. It’s definitely not a -2 song, but there are few duds this week.
This is a bit of a binary one. I suspect it won’t go down too well, but it brings instant flashbacks of my years at art school (well, architecture, but it’s the same). I’ll allow it! But no perfume.
It’s good. Still bo perfume.
A healthy dose of nihilism to start the week. I love it! It’s almost Jaques Brelle’sque! No perfume.
That nasal croaking does not help the limp humour in a song absolutely not about perfume. This would be bad enough without the attempted country.
We’ve done nihilism, and now it’s time for the monday morning dada. No perfume but excellent nevertheless.
Agreed. It’s very catchy,
The mention of polar bears made me remember, that I have to zero my rifle, so I can snipe at the invading Americans. Good song.
It’s beatiful. No perfume, but mentions of things that smell, like salt sea strand and roses, even if their scent is not mentioned. But just a beautiful song.
It’s fine, but no perfume.
Not bad, but a bit plodding, no perfume and it’s just not quite there in a week with no truly bad songs.
I like this dancey track. Second one this week. No perfume, but a beat you can dance to and a good melody.
A sparkle on the water, no scents though. It’s fine.
This might be about perfume. Who knows? (the Spanish knows). The song is nice enough, but I’m not blown away.

It’s not bad. Avec apologies.

Not bad at all!
Best intro of the week but that's not enough to save this one. Pretty bad - A bit of a dirge. I think this will do well but it's not for me. Downvoted with apologies.

Yes, I am awarding points to Neil Hamburger. No, no one is more embarrassed about this than myself. But in my meager defense, it's funny and works on both literal and metaphorical levels. You win this time, Satan.

Perfect followup to Disappointment. One of my all time favorite songs. Please play this at my funeral.
